name.description: Stable display-layer skill identifier. Lowercase kebab-case; allows `/` and `:` for hierarchical/namespaced names. Must equal the parent directory name (skills/<name>/SKILL.md). The URN at `urn` is the long-term globally-unique identifier; `name` is the local handle.

description.description: A short description of what the skill is about. Activation, trigger, and boundary semantics belong to the dedicated fields built for them: `keywords` and `triggers` for activation signals, `examples` and `anti_examples` for prompt-level coverage, and `relations.boundary` for routing-layer exclusion edges. Keep `description` descriptive, not prescriptive. No protocol length cap.

taxonomy_domain.description: Hierarchical taxonomy sub-path using slash-delimited segments (e.g., `ecommerce/integrations/shopify`). Complements `subject`: the flat browse shelf (`subject`) and the slash-delimited taxonomy tree answer different questions. Use `taxonomy_domain` to subdivide a subject that holds many skills (the >25 rule per `subject`'s balance constraint). Renamed from `domain` to disambiguate from `grounding.subject_matter` (the grounding-block free-text label) and from cross-taxonomy routing doctrine prose. See the ADR-0017 amendment of 2026-05-27.

subject.description: Primary classification ŌĆö the competency the skill teaches ("what does this teach you to do?"). Closed 12-value enum in 3 navigational bands: software & web engineering (backend-engineering, frontend-engineering, software-architecture, data-engineering); AI-agentic (agent-ops, ai-engineering); cross-cutting craft (quality-assurance, design, reasoning-strategy, software-engineering-method, knowledge-organization, product-domain). Balance rule: each subject must hold 5-25 skills. <5 = fold or recruit; >25 = subdivide via `taxonomy_domain` slash-path. See docs/adr/0020-twelve-shelf-competency-reaxis.md for the current shelf rationale.

subjects.description: Ordered subject array for polyhierarchy. First entry is the primary and must match `subject`; second entry (optional) is a secondary subject for skills that genuinely span shelves (e.g. `webhook-integration` is primarily `backend-engineering` and secondarily `product-domain`; `information-architecture` is primarily `design` and secondarily `knowledge-organization`). Max 2 entries to keep polyhierarchy the exception, not the default. Drawn from the same closed 12-enum as `subject`.

scope.description: PRD-style free-text scope statement ŌĆö what this skill teaches and what it does not. Mirrors the body `## Coverage` plus `## Do NOT Use When` sections at the frontmatter level for fast scanning. Required. NOT an enum: the deployment-targeting role moved to `deployment_target` (with the `workspace` value removed). See the ADR-0017 amendment of 2026-05-27.

public.description: Publishability gate ŌĆö is this skill safe for public release to the skills.sh marketplace? `true` (publishable: carries no private data ŌĆö repo-agnostic patterns, public-repo grounding, or otherwise leak-free), `false` (private: carries private API keys, personal data, customer data, bank/financial data, or internal-only operational doctrine and must NEVER be published). This is the SINGLE switch that drives the marketplace-export filter (`export-marketplace-skills.js` excludes `public: false`) and enforces the HARD private-content boundary (see `~/.claude-profiles/.../memory/skill-graph-private-content-boundary`). Replaces the v8 `deployment_target` enum: publishability ŌĆö not deployment location ŌĆö is what the export gate actually needs; project-grounding is carried separately by `grounding` + `project[]`. Migration alias: `deployment_target: portable` ŌåÆ `public: true`, `deployment_target: project` ŌåÆ `public: false` (the conservative default ŌĆö a project-grounded skill is assumed private until the audit loop confirms otherwise). See the ADR-0017 amendment.

project.description: Projects this skill is linked to. Each entry has a kebab-case `handle` and a free-text `role` (suggested values: `source-of-truth`, `consumer`, `mirror`). Optional; absent = ambient / cross-project. Replaces the v8 `workspace_tags` field and makes project belonging-entity identity a first-class queryable axis. See the ADR-0017 amendment of 2026-05-27.

role.description: Free-text role of this skill within the project. Suggested values: `source-of-truth`, `consumer`, `mirror`.

mental_model.description: Primitives and their relationships. Name primitives and the relationships between them. Markdown permitted. Author with the depth the concept needs ŌĆö no protocol length cap. Authored input read by the comprehension grader's `mental_model` dimension (weight 1.5). Replaces nested `concept.mental_model` in v6 ŌĆö see the v5-to-v6 migration note.

purpose.description: What problem the concept solves and the alternative it replaced. Concrete pain point + prior alternative. No length cap. Authored input read by the comprehension grader's `purpose` dimension (weight 1.0). Replaces nested `concept.purpose` in v6.

concept_boundary.description: Things commonly confused with the concept but that are NOT it. Express each difference as a mechanism (different primitives, purpose, or scope) ŌĆö not just different names. No length cap. Authored input read by the comprehension grader's boundary dimension (weight 1.5). Canonical name as of ADR-0018, which renamed the top-level Understanding field from `boundary` to `concept_boundary` so the token no longer collides with the routing-layer `relations.suppresses` (formerly `relations.boundary`). New skills author `concept_boundary`.

boundary.description: DEPRECATED alias of `concept_boundary` (renamed per ADR-0018 to remove the token collision with the routing edge `relations.boundary`/`relations.suppresses`). Retained ONLY for skills not yet migrated; the normalizer presents it as `concept_boundary` and `concept_boundary` wins when both are present. New skills MUST author `concept_boundary`. Structural removal of this alias is CONTENT-mode work the audit loop drains per-skill.

analogy.description: Analogy that preserves the core mechanism. Translates the concept for a non-expert without breaking the structural relationship between primitives. No length cap. Authored input read by the comprehension grader's `analogy` dimension (weight 0.5). Replaces nested `concept.analogy` in v6.

misconception.description: The wrong mental model people bring and why it misleads. Authored hint to inoculate the agent against the common error trap. No length cap. Not directly graded; complements `boundary`. Replaces nested `concept.misconception` in v6.

stability.description: Lifecycle posture for consumers. `experimental` (subject to change), `stable` (production-ready), `frozen` (no further changes expected), `deprecated` (use `superseded_by` to name the replacement). Drives consumer pinning decisions.

superseded_by.description: Name of the skill that replaces this one. Required when `stability: deprecated` ŌĆö enforced by the allOf rule so every deprecated skill names its successor and consumers can follow the chain automatically. Omit on non-deprecated skills.

license.description: SPDX license identifier (e.g., `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`, `CC-BY-4.0`). Resolved against the SPDX license list (ISO/IEC 5962:2021). Required for skills shipped externally; optional for codebase-internal skills.

runtimes.description: Target agent runtimes with optional version constraints (e.g., `claude-code>=2.0`, `cursor>=0.40`). `agent_runtimes` is the v3.1 preferred/canonical name (it disambiguates from the Node/container runtime); `runtimes` is the legacy alias. When both are present they must match.

agent_runtimes.description: Target agent runtimes (v3.1 preferred alias for `runtimes`). Disambiguates from Node runtime, container runtime, etc. When both are present they must match.

node.description: Node.js version constraint (e.g., `>=18`). `node_version` is the v3.1 preferred/canonical name (it disambiguates from `node` reading as a graph-node in a graph repo); `node` is the legacy alias. When both are present they must match.

node_version.description: Node.js version constraint (v3.1 preferred alias for `node`). Disambiguates from `node` reading as graph-node in a graph repo. When both are present they must match.

notes.description: Free-text additional compatibility notes. Use sparingly; prefer structured fields. No protocol length cap.

compatibility.description: Cross-runtime compatibility envelope. `runtimes` lists target agent runtimes with version constraints; `node` is the Node.js version requirement; `notes` is free-text overflow. Distinct from `relations.depends_on` (sibling skill dependency).

allowed-tools.description: Optional space-separated whitelist of tools the skill is permitted to use (e.g., `Read Edit Bash`). Honoured by harnesses that gate tool calls per skill. Kebab-case spelling matches the common SKILL.md field name and Claude Code's `--allowed-tools` CLI flag. The canonical key is `allowed-tools` (kebab-case); `allowed_tools` (snake_case) is only a tolerated read alias the normalizer accepts ŌĆö author the kebab form. The export transform writes the kebab-case form for SKILL.md consumers.

triggers.description: Exact phrase or label triggers that activate this skill. For semantic phrases use `keywords`; for example user prompts use `examples`.

keywords.description: Semantic keywords for discovery and fuzzy matching. Complements `triggers` (exact) and `examples` (full prompts).

examples.description: Positive-class activation examples ŌĆö realistic user prompts the skill SHOULD activate for. 2ŌĆō5 entries recommended. Improves recall for embedding-based routers (SkillRouter-style few-shot retrieval) that find `keywords` alone insufficient at library scale. Groups under `activation.examples` in the manifest.

anti_examples.description: Negative-class activation examples ŌĆö realistic user prompts that look topically related but a DIFFERENT skill should handle. Used as hard-negative training signal. Pair with `relations.boundary` to name the skill that should activate instead. Groups under `activation.anti_examples` in the manifest.

paths.description: Glob patterns that identify code surfaces this skill governs. Patterns prefixed with `!` are negations (gitignore-style). Negations only subtract from prior includes; a pattern list consisting only of negations matches nothing and is rejected by lint.

relations.description: Typed edges to sibling skills. Lint verifies every target exists. Predicate-to-W3C-vocabulary mapping is provided via schemas/skill.context.jsonld (JSON-LD @context). `suppresses` is the routing-layer asymmetric exclusion edge (renamed from the deprecated `boundary` alias per ADR-0018); `disjoint_with` is the optional OWL class-disjointness predicate ŌĆö they are distinct, not aliases.

adjacent.description: Legacy alias of `related` (skos:related). Retained ONLY for skills that have not yet been migrated to the canonical `related` name; new skills MUST author `related`. Per AGENTS.md ┬¦ Major Version Is a Clean Cut, structural removal of this alias is CONTENT-mode work the audit loop drains per-skill.

related.description: Symmetric associative relation (skos:related). Use for skills that should be co-read because they teach the same surface from different angles. Max 5 entries recommended to avoid hub-and-spoke clutter. Preferred over the deprecated `adjacent` alias.

suppresses.description: Score-aware routing exclusion edge ŌĆö directional. Skills this skill excludes from co-routing when this skill wins or ties; this is NOT a defer-to-target pointer ("skill-A suppresses skill-B" reads as "when skill-A wins, skill-B is suppressed from the result set", which IS the runtime mechanic). Canonical name as of ADR-0018, which renamed the routing field from `boundary` so the name matches the mechanic. Items may be bare skill names or `{skill, reason}` objects; reasons should use ownership wording. Maps to sg:disjointOwnership ŌĆö a Skill-Graph-specific routing-layer predicate, intentionally weaker than OWL class-disjointness. For formal class-disjointness, use `disjoint_with`.

boundary.description: DEPRECATED alias of `suppresses` (renamed per ADR-0018 ŌĆö the name `boundary` read as deference but the mechanic is exclusion). Retained ONLY for skills not yet migrated to the canonical `suppresses` name; new skills MUST author `suppresses`. Per AGENTS.md ┬¦ Major Version Is a Clean Cut, structural removal of this alias is CONTENT-mode work the audit loop drains per-skill. Same mechanic, same item shape as `suppresses`; the router reads `suppresses` first and falls back to `boundary`.

disjoint_with.description: Optional OWL class-disjointness assertion. Maps to owl:disjointWith ŌĆö a formal ontological claim that the two skills name disjoint conceptual classes (no entity can simultaneously be an instance of both). Rare in practice. Does NOT replace `boundary` (the routing-layer asymmetric exclusion edge). Same item shape as `boundary` ŌĆö bare string OR `{skill, reason}`.

broader.description: Cross-skill generalisation (skos:broader). Use when this skill is a specialisation of another, more general skill. Example: `react-best-practices` has `broader: [frontend]`. Closes the cross-skill generalisation gap that the flat `subject`/`taxonomy_domain` taxonomy cannot express.

narrower.description: Cross-skill specialisation (skos:narrower). The inverse of `broader` ŌĆö this skill is more general than the targets. Tooling can infer this from other skills' `broader` edges; authoring it explicitly is optional.

verify_with.description: Skills to co-load for verification. Maps to prov:wasInformedBy ŌĆö this skill's claims are informed by the verifier's claims. Keep to 1-3 high-signal verifiers.

depends_on.description: Skills this skill requires conceptually or operationally. Items may be bare skill names or `{skill, min_version}` objects for version-constrained dependencies.

min_version.description: Semver range for the minimum required version of the target skill. Accepts bare semver (`1.2.0`, treated as exact), caret range (`^1.2.0`), tilde range (`~1.2.0`), comparator range (`>=1.2.0`, `>1.2.0`, `<2.0.0`, `<=1.5.0`), or AND-joined ranges (`>=1.2.0 <2.0.0`).

io_contract.description: OPTIONAL machine-checkable composition contract. Declares the abstract artifact TYPES this skill consumes (`inputs`) and produces (`outputs`) as a deterministic, LLM-free composition signal. Type tokens are kebab-case abstract nouns naming a kind of artifact (e.g. `skill-md`, `audit-findings`, `manifest`, `routing-config`), NOT concrete file paths. The builder derives `depends_on` edges from outputŌåÆinput compatibility (skill B depends_on skill A when A's outputs intersect B's inputs) ŌĆö the same machine-checkable composition pattern as Graph of Skills (arXiv 2604.05333) and SkillNet (arXiv 2603.04448). When absent, the skill simply contributes no I/O-derived edges; this field is opt-in and forces no corpus migration. Composition validity (broken chains: an authored `depends_on` target whose outputs do not satisfy this skill's inputs) and cycles on the `depends_on` subgraph are checked by `scripts/skill/check-io-composition.js`. Distinct from `depends_on` (which names specific sibling skills) ŌĆö `io_contract` names artifact TYPES and lets the tooling discover the skills.

inputs.description: Abstract artifact types this skill consumes to do its job. kebab-case type tokens, not file paths. A skill that consumes audit findings and a manifest declares `inputs: [audit-findings, manifest]`.

outputs.description: Abstract artifact types this skill produces. kebab-case type tokens, not file paths. A skill that produces audit findings declares `outputs: [audit-findings]`; another skill listing `audit-findings` in its `inputs` is then derivable as depending on this one.

subject_matter.description: Free-text label naming what the skill is grounded in (e.g., `Shopify order sync`, `Skill Metadata Protocol and Skill Graph manifest consistency`). Renamed from v8 `grounding.domain_object`; the v3.1 `grounding.subject` alias is retired. See the ADR-0017 amendment of 2026-05-27.

grounding_mode.description: Whether the skill's claims are repo-specific, universal, or a hybrid. `claim_scope` is the supported alias.

claim_scope.description: Whether the skill's claims are repo-specific, universal, or a hybrid (v3.1 preferred alias for `grounding_mode`). When both are present they must match.

oneOf.description: Legacy whole-resource reference. Local paths are resolved relative to the repo root; URLs are allowed for external specs.

path.description: Local file path or URL for the truth source.

line_range.description: Optional inclusive line range to hash and review instead of the whole file.

anchor.description: Optional stable heading slug or literal text anchor used by lint to detect broken pointers.

note.description: Short human note explaining why this source grounds the skill.

truth_sources.description: Files, docs, or URLs that ground the skill's claims. Prefer object entries with path plus line_range or anchor for repo-backed skills; string entries remain valid for v3 compatibility.

grounding.description: Records what the skill is grounded against ŌĆö the truth sources, the grounding mode, and the failure modes when the truth drifts. Required when the skill makes claims about specific code or external systems. Optional for purely conceptual skills.

allOf._comment: A project-anchored skill (non-empty project[]) must ground its claims in that project's truth sources. Re-anchored from the retired deployment_target: project rule ŌĆö grounding follows project membership, not the publishability gate (public).
Polyhierarchy prefix rule: when both subject (singular) and subjects (array) are present, subjects[0] MUST equal subject.
